content.text: Sprint 08 — Studio route. I'll start by inspecting the tree state and reading the files this sprint composes from (Work's pattern, the motion primitives, data modules, QA scripts), then build S1/S2/S3, extend the QA suite, run the gate with a fresh-eyes visual audit workflow, and commit.

6	    "description": "We learn your domain before we write a line of code. What's broken? What's manual? What would change if an agent handled it? We scope the whole problem, not a ticket."
7	  },
8	  {
9	    "num": "02",
10	    "title": "Build Complete",
11	    "description": "We finish the whole implementation. Not a sprint slice. Not a demo. The actual working system. A half-shipped feature tells you nothing."
12	  },
13	  {
14	    "num": "03",
15	    "title": "Ship & Measure",
16	    "description": "Every release is a question, not a statement. We put it in front of real users, real workflows, real edge cases. Then we watch what happens."
17	  },
18	  {
19	    "num": "04",
20	    "title": "Feedback & Iterate",

content.text: Tree is clean, S01–S07 all PASS. Now reading the motion primitives and a Work section for the composition pattern.

content.content:
1 /** 2 * W2 — Industry-solutions skyline (Work) — the [data-showpiece="skyline"] act. 3 * 4 * ============================== S12 CONTRACT =============================== 5 * - Mount: [data-showpiece="skyline"] (below), position:relative. S12 MAY 6 * attach the instanced GL extrude as an `absolute inset-0` child of this 7 * mount — ONE instanced draw (instanceMatrix/instanceColor, never 9 meshes, 8 * <= 40 draw calls), lazy chunk only, desktop full tier only. 9 * - Data binding: the 9 cards bind src/data/industries.js (INDUSTRIES — 9 of 10 * {industry,stat,subtitle,before,result,after}). Each card root carries 11 * data-industry="" so GL instances map 1:1 to cards. 12 * - THE DOM card-field below ([data-skyline-field]) IS the lite/none design: 13 * reduced-motion + mobile render it static with before/after both shown and 14 * final stat numbers (scar #8 — never a blank). S12 must KEEP it as the 15 * fallback and gate the canvas off it, not replace it. 16 * - Any pin/scrub S12 adds registers via useRouteScrollContext().add() 17 * (scar #9) with a scar-#5 latch; ZERO `three` bytes on lite/none tiers. 18 * =========================================================================== 19 * 20 * This sprint is pure DOM/GSAP (no three imports anywhere on Work). Travel 21 * vectors: the 9 volumes RISE (InkReveal from="up", diagonal stagger) while 22 * each 3-card row counter-PANS horizontally (ParallaxLayer axis="x", 23 * alternating direction per row — the field drifts like a panned drafting 24 * sheet). Stats + result figures CountStrike to their locked values; the 25 * non-numeric stats render static. Locked facts (9 names + 9 stats + 9 26 * results) come from src/data/industries.js, never hand-typed. 27 */ 28 import { INDUSTRIES } from "../../data/industries.js"; 29 import { 30 Section, 31 SectionHeader, 32 InkReveal, 33 ParallaxLayer, 34 CountStrike, 35 } from "../../components/motion/index.js"; 36 37 function slugify(s) { 38 return s 39 .toLowerCase() 40 .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "-") 41 .replace(/(^-|-$)/g, ""); 42 } 43 44 /** 45 * Numeric-leading figures count-strike to their exact locked string 46 * ("98% defect-free output" → to=98, suffix="% defect-free output"); 47 * non-numeric figures ("Real-time fraud detection") render static. 48 * The DOM always ships the final verbatim string either way. 49 */ 50 function Figure({ value, className = "", start }) { 51 const m = /^(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)(.*)$/.exec(value); 52 if (!m) return {value}; 53 const decimals = (m[1].split(".")[1] || "").length; 54 return ( 55 62 ); 63 } 64 65 // 3 rows × 3 volumes — the row is the pan unit, the card is the rise unit. 66 const ROWS = [INDUSTRIES.slice(0, 3), INDUSTRIES.slice(3, 6), INDUSTRIES.slice(6, 9)]; 67 68 export default function SkylineField() { 69 return ( 70

content.content: 1 /** 2 * Section / SectionHeader — the layout & rhythm primitive every page sprint 3 * (S06–S13) composes. Deliberately STATIC: it owns no GSAP, no ScrollTrigger, 4 * no reveal of its own. Page sprints wrap their content in InkReveal/SplitLines 5 * themselves, so keeping Section animation-free decouples the layout tier from 6 * the motion tier — Section never decides when content reveals. 7 * 8 * Section props: 9 * id string — anchor id forwarded to the root element. 10 * as elem — element/tag to render as (default "section"). 11 * padded bool — when true, vertical section padding via the 12 * --k-section-pad token (default true). 13 * bordered bool — when true, a bottom hairline (border-b border-line). 14 * className string — merged onto the root, after the guaranteed utilities. 15 * children node — section body (placed inside the centered container). 16 * ...rest — spread onto the root element. 17 * 18 * SectionHeader props: 19 * eyebrow string — optional mono micro-label above the title. 20 * title string — the h2 headline (required for a meaningful header). 21 * lead string — optional ink-dim supporting paragraph under the title. 22 * className string — merged onto the header root. 23 * 24 * Gate: NONE. There is no motion to gate; Section renders identically on every 25 * tier. It is the host other primitives reveal *inside*. 26 * 27 * Reduced / mobile fallback: identical to the default render (no motion path). 28 * 29 * Scar notes: 30 * - Scar #4: the root is ALWAYS `relative` so any absolute inset-0 child a 31 * page drops inside is positioned against the Section, not the viewport. 32 * - No injected

1 /** 2 * W3 — Recap (Work). Ties the two case files + nine verticals back into one 3 * ask and routes to /contact. Travel vector: DIAGONAL counter-drift — a 4 * drafting title-block stamp drifts against scroll on nested y✕x 5 * ParallaxLayers (the HomeHero idiom) while the content strikes in from the 6 * left. Primary CTA = MagneticButton rendering a real router to 7 * /contact (verify.mjs clicks it and asserts the route change); secondary = 8 * the locked #newsletter reach via the S05 goToNewsletter helper (never 9 * hand-roll the scroll). 10 */ 11 import { Link } from "react-router-dom"; 12 import { LINKS } from "../../data/links.js"; 13 import { CASE_STUDIES } from "../../data/caseStudies.js"; 14 import { INDUSTRIES } from "../../data/industries.js"; 15 import { goToNewsletter } from "../../lib/goToNewsletter.js"; 16 import { 17 Section, 18 SplitLines, 19 InkReveal, 20 MagneticButton, 21 ParallaxLayer, 22 } from "../../components/motion/index.js"; 23 24 export default function RecapDrift() { 25 return ( 26
